query:
You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.1714 (commit: 7c30e125).

## Issue to Resolve

Update to Anki 23.12 backend
The Anki update includes some significant changes to the FSRS algorithm. So, for best results, all reviews should be done on apps with the same codebase.

(There are no "breaking" changes, but still ...)

Now that the 23.12 release is public, the backend code of AnkiDroid should be updated as soon as possible.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I/we get notifications for anki releases, we are aware of them for what it's worth
I was almost certain that you guys get notifications for the releases, but I still created this issue because the 23.12 update changes the scheduler code, which means that doing reviews on apps with different backend versions can lead to some unwanted behaviour. So, quickly updating to this version is somewhat more important.

After this release, for several months, there are no expected changes to the FSRS scheduler code. So, I would not urge for a very rapid update to the new backend version for those releases.
Also, 23.12 introduced some important fixes. From release notes:

- When cards are rescheduled multiple times without reviews, extra entries will not be added to the review log.
- Fix some bugs in sorting by FSRS retrievability.
- Recompute FSRS memory state when cards moved into different deck.
- Added a new v3 option to limit child decks by their parent, when clicking on the child.
- Fix filtered deck ordering of intraday learning cards and reviews
- Fix relative overdueness order error in filtered deck.
The new backend can't be simply dropped in. Some changes will need to be made to AnkiDroid:

- The filtered deck screen will need to support customizing the separate preview delays for again/hard & good buttons
- The auto advance feature will need to switch to using the settings in the current card's deck options
> The new backend can't be simply dropped in.

Yes, I agree. All that I have to say is this update should take a slightly higher priority than normal.
Another issue I didn't mention above: Anki displays various options when importing an .apkg file, which AnkiDroid is currently not doing. As a temporary workaround to get things to compile when switching to the 23.12 backend, you can hard-code the argument change:

```diff
diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
index 07ec516536..bb272bd17b 100644
--- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
+++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@
 package com.ichi2.libanki

 import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
+import anki.import_export.exportAnkiPackageOptions
 import anki.search.SearchNode
 import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend

@@ -107,7 +108,13 @@ fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
     limit: ExportLimit,
     legacy: Boolean = true
 ) {
-    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
+    val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
+        this.withScheduling = withScheduling
+        this.withMedia = withMedia
+        this.legacy = legacy
+        this.withDeckConfigs = true
+    }
+    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
 }

 fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
diff --git a/build.gradle b/build.gradle
index aeafc762f8..2057d8a32f 100644
--- a/build.gradle
+++ b/build.gradle
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ buildscript {
     ext.kotlin_version = '1.9.21'
     ext.lint_version = '31.1.1'
     ext.acra_version = '5.11.3'
-    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.32-anki23.10.1'
+    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.34-anki23.12'
     ext.hamcrest_version = '2.2'
     ext.junit_version = '5.10.1'
     ext.coroutines_version = '1.7.3'
```

A proper fix will mean using the import-anki-package.html page. Currently, for some unknown reason, it is not being included in the backend builds, and it could use the help of someone knowledgeable in Gradle to debug.

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android-Backend/pull/339

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/card_template.html`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/BackendImporting.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiPackageImporterFragment.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiServer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/PostRequestHandler.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerViewModel.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt`
- `build.gradle`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 17â†’package com.ichi2.libanki
 18â†’
 19â†’import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
 20â†’import anki.search.SearchNode
 21â†’import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend
 22â†’
 23â†’/**
 24â†’ * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 25â†’ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 26â†’ * and this routine will return false.
 27â†’ *
 28â†’ * There must not be an active transaction.
 29â†’ *
 30â†’ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 31â†’ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 32â†’ * to check for success.
 33â†’ *
 34â†’ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 35â†’ *
 36â†’ */
 37â†’fun Collection.createBackup(
 38â†’    backupFolder: String,
 39â†’    force: Boolean,
 40â†’    waitForCompletion: Boolean
 41â†’): Boolean {
 42â†’    return backend.createBackup(
 43â†’        backupFolder = backupFolder,
 44â†’        force = force,
 45â†’        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion
 46â†’    )
 47â†’}
 48â†’
 49â†’/**
 50â†’ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 51â†’ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 52â†’ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 53â†’ */
 54â†’fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
 55â†’    backend.awaitBackupCompletion()
 56â†’}
 57â†’
 58â†’/**
 59â†’ * Replace the collection file with the one in the provided .colpkg file.
 60â†’ * The collection must be already closed, and must be opened afterwards.
 61â†’ * */
 62â†’fun importCollectionPackage(
 63â†’    backend: Backend,
 64â†’    colPath: String,
 65â†’    colpkgPath: String
 66â†’) {
 67â†’    backend.importCollectionPackage(
 68â†’        colPath = colPath,
 69â†’        backupPath = colpkgPath,
 70â†’        mediaFolder = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media"),
 71â†’        mediaDb = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media.db")
 72â†’    )
 73â†’}
 74â†’
 75â†’/**
 76â†’ * Export the collection into a .colpkg file.
 77â†’ * If legacy=false, a file targeting Anki 2.1.50+ is created. It compresses better and is faster to
 78â†’ * create, but older clients can not read it.
 79â†’ */
 80â†’fun Collection.exportCollectionPackage(
 81â†’    outPath: String,
 82â†’    includeMedia: Boolean,
 83â†’    legacy: Boolean = true
 84â†’) {
 85â†’    close(forFullSync = true)
 86â†’    backend.exportCollectionPackage(
 87â†’        outPath = outPath,
 88â†’        includeMedia = includeMedia,
 89â†’        legacy = legacy
 90â†’    )
 91â†’    reopen()
 92â†’}
 93â†’
 94â†’fun Collection.importAnkiPackageRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
 95â†’    return backend.importAnkiPackageRaw(input)
 96â†’}
 97â†’
 98â†’/**
 99â†’ * Export the specified deck to an .apkg file.
100â†’ * * If legacy is false, an apkg will be created that can only
101â†’ * be opened with recent Anki versions.
102â†’ */
103â†’fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
104â†’    outPath: String,
105â†’    withScheduling: Boolean,
106â†’    withMedia: Boolean,
107â†’    limit: ExportLimit,
108â†’    legacy: Boolean = true
109â†’) {
110â†’    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
111â†’}
112â†’
113â†’fun Collection.exportNotesCsv(
114â†’    outPath: String,
115â†’    withHtml: Boolean,
116â†’    withTags: Boolean,
117â†’    withDeck: Boolean,
118â†’    withNotetype: Boolean,
119â†’    withGuid: Boolean,
120â†’    limit: ExportLimit
121â†’) {
122â†’    backend.exportNoteCsv(outPath, withHtml, withTags, withDeck, withNotetype, withGuid, limit)
123â†’}
124â†’
125â†’fun Collection.exportCardsCsv(outPath: String, withHtml: Boolean, limit: ExportLimit) {
126â†’    backend.exportCardCsv(outPath, withHtml, limit)
127â†’}
128â†’
129â†’fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
130â†’    return backend.getCsvMetadataRaw(input)
131â†’}
132â†’
133â†’fun Collection.importCsvRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
134â†’    return backend.importCsvRaw(input)
135â†’}
136â†’
137â†’fun Collection.buildSearchString(input: ByteArray): String {
138â†’    return backend.buildSearchString(SearchNode.parseFrom(input))
139â†’}

 14â†’ *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.

 17â†’package com.ichi2.anki.reviewer
 18â†’
 19â†’import android.content.SharedPreferences
 20â†’import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 21â†’import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 22â†’import com.ichi2.anki.R
 23â†’import com.ichi2.anki.Reviewer
 24â†’import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
 25â†’import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.AnswerButtons.*
 26â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 27â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.DeckId
 28â†’import com.ichi2.utils.HandlerUtils
 29â†’import timber.log.Timber
 30â†’
 31â†’/**
 32â†’ * AnkiDroid contains a setting: "Automatic display answer" which displays the
 33â†’ * question/answer automatically after a set period of time.
 34â†’ *
 35â†’ * ## Settings
 36â†’ * See: [AutomaticAnswerSettings]
 37â†’ *
 38â†’ * ## Implementation
 39â†’ *
 40â†’ * If "Enabled" is disabled, then nothing happens
 41â†’ * If Time to show Answer is 0, the automatic flipping code is disabled
 42â†’ * If Time to show next question is 0, the automatic answering code is disabled
 43â†’ *
 44â†’ * When a card is displayed (Front)
 45â†’ *
 46â†’ * If it has neither TTS nor audio, schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 47â†’ * If it has audio, calculate the time for the audio to play
 48â†’ *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" + "audio runtime" seconds
 49â†’ * If it has TTS, add a handler to the completion of TTS
 50â†’ *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 51â†’ *
 52â†’ * Do the same for the Back, but use the "Time to show next question" setting.
 53â†’ *
 54â†’ * If the "next question" is shown, "Again" is pressed.
 55â†’ *
 56â†’ * The implementation originally had multiple bugs regarding double presses of the answer buttons.
 57â†’ * This is because the user can press show the card at the same instant that the automatic code executes
 58â†’ *
 59â†’ * This has been worked around via the calls: [onSelectEase] and [onShowAnswer].
 60â†’ * Both of which clear the task queue for the question ([onSelectEase] or vice-versa)
 61â†’ */
 62â†’class AutomaticAnswer(
 63â†’    target: AutomaticallyAnswered,
 64â†’    @VisibleForTesting val settings: AutomaticAnswerSettings
 65â†’) {
 66â†’
 67â†’    /** Whether any tasks should be executed/scheduled.
 68â†’     *
 69â†’     * Ensures that auto answer does not occur if the reviewer is minimised
 70â†’     */
 71â†’    var isDisabled: Boolean = false
 72â†’        private set
 73â†’
 74â†’    /**
 75â†’     * Whether the sounds for the current side of the card have been played
 76â†’     * So [scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer] and [scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion] do nothing
 77â†’     * if called multiple times on the same side of the card
 78â†’     */
 79â†’    private var hasPlayedSounds: Boolean = false
 80â†’
 81â†’    private val showAnswerTask = Runnable {
 82â†’        if (isDisabled) {
 83â†’            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
 84â†’            return@Runnable
 85â†’        }
 86â†’        target.automaticShowAnswer()
 87â†’    }
 88â†’    private val showQuestionTask = Runnable {
 89â†’        if (isDisabled) {
 90â†’            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
 91â†’            return@Runnable
 92â†’        }
 93â†’        target.automaticShowQuestion(settings.answerAction)
 94â†’    }
 95â†’
 96â†’    /**
 97â†’     * Handler for the delay in auto showing question and/or answer
 98â†’     * One toggle for both question and answer, could set longer delay for auto next question
 99â†’     */
100â†’    @VisibleForTesting
101â†’    val timeoutHandler = HandlerUtils.newHandler()
102â†’
103â†’    @VisibleForTesting
104â†’    fun delayedShowQuestion(delay: Long) {
105â†’        if (isDisabled) {
106â†’            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
107â†’            return
108â†’        }
109â†’        Timber.i("Automatically showing question in %dms", delay)
110â†’        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showQuestionTask, delay)
111â†’    }
112â†’
113â†’    @VisibleForTesting
114â†’    fun delayedShowAnswer(delay: Long) {
115â†’        if (isDisabled) {
116â†’            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
117â†’            return
118â†’        }
119â†’        Timber.i("Automatically showing answer in %dms", delay)
120â†’        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showAnswerTask, delay)
121â†’    }
122â†’
123â†’    private fun stopShowQuestionTask() {
124â†’        Timber.i("stop: automatically show question")
125â†’        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showQuestionTask)
126â†’    }
127â†’
128â†’    private fun stopShowAnswerTask() {
129â†’        Timber.i("stop: automatically show answer")
130â†’        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showAnswerTask)
131â†’    }
132â†’
133â†’    fun enable() {
134â†’        isDisabled = false
135â†’    }
136â†’
137â†’    fun disable() {
138â†’        isDisabled = true
139â†’        stopShowAnswerTask()
140â†’        stopShowQuestionTask()
141â†’    }
142â†’
143â†’    /** Stop any "Automatic show answer" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
144â†’    fun onDisplayQuestion() {
145â†’        if (!settings.useTimer) return
146â†’        if (!settings.autoAdvanceAnswer) return
147â†’        hasPlayedSounds = false
148â†’
149â†’        stopShowAnswerTask()
150â†’    }
151â†’
152â†’    /** Stop any "Automatic show question" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
153â†’    fun onDisplayAnswer() {
154â†’        if (!settings.useTimer) return
155â†’        if (!settings.autoAdvanceQuestion) return
156â†’        hasPlayedSounds = false
157â†’
158â†’        stopShowQuestionTask()
159â†’    }
160â†’
161â†’    // region TODO: These attempt to stop a race condition between a manual answer and the automated answer
162â†’    // I don't believe this is thread-safe
163â†’
164â†’    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has selected an answer */
165â†’    fun onSelectEase() {
166â†’        Timber.v("onSelectEase")
167â†’        stopShowQuestionTask()
168â†’    }
169â†’
170â†’    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has flipped the card */
171â†’    fun onShowAnswer() {
172â†’        Timber.v("onShowAnswer")
173â†’        stopShowAnswerTask()
174â†’    }
175â†’
176â†’    // endregion
177â†’
178â†’    /**
179â†’     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowAnswer]
180â†’     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
181â†’     */
182â†’    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer(additionalDelay: Long = 0) {
183â†’        if (!settings.useTimer) return
184â†’        if (!settings.autoAdvanceAnswer) return
185â†’        if (hasPlayedSounds) return
186â†’        hasPlayedSounds = true
187â†’        delayedShowAnswer(settings.answerDelayMilliseconds + additionalDelay)
188â†’    }
189â†’
190â†’    /**
191â†’     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowQuestion]
192â†’     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
193â†’     */
194â†’    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion(additionalMediaDelay: Long = 0) {
195â†’        if (!settings.useTimer) return
196â†’        if (!settings.autoAdvanceQuestion) return
197â†’        if (hasPlayedSounds) return
198â†’        hasPlayedSounds = true
199â†’        delayedShowQuestion(settings.questionDelayMilliseconds + additionalMediaDelay)
200â†’    }
201â†’
202â†’    fun isEnabled(): Boolean {
203â†’        return settings.useTimer
204â†’    }
205â†’
206â†’    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
207â†’    internal fun simulateCardFlip() {
208â†’        Timber.d("simulateCardFlip")
209â†’        hasPlayedSounds = false
210â†’    }
211â†’
212â†’    interface AutomaticallyAnswered {
213â†’        fun automaticShowAnswer()
214â†’        fun automaticShowQuestion(action: AutomaticAnswerAction)
215â†’    }
216â†’
217â†’    companion object {
218â†’        @CheckResult
219â†’        fun defaultInstance(target: AutomaticallyAnswered): AutomaticAnswer {
220â†’            return AutomaticAnswer(target, AutomaticAnswerSettings())
221â†’        }
222â†’
223â†’        @CheckResult
224â†’        fun createInstance(target: AutomaticallyAnswered, preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswer {
225â†’            val settings = AutomaticAnswerSettings.createInstance(preferences, col)
226â†’            return AutomaticAnswer(target, settings)
227â†’        }
228â†’    }
229â†’}
230â†’
231â†’/**
232â†’ * ### Reviewing - Automatic display answer
233â†’ *
234â†’ * * Enabled
235â†’ * * Time to show answer (20s)
236â†’ * * Time to show next question (60s)
237â†’ * * Automatic action: the action to take when reviewing
238â†’ *
239â†’ * ### Deck Options - Reviews - Automatic Display Answer
240â†’ *
241â†’ * These settings also exist in an options group
242â†’ * Although they're under "reviews", it counts for all cards
243â†’ *
244â†’ * * Use General settings (using the settings above - enabled by default)
245â†’ * * Enabled, etc...
246â†’ */
247â†’class AutomaticAnswerSettings(
248â†’    val answerAction: AutomaticAnswerAction = AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD,
249â†’    @get:JvmName("useTimer") val useTimer: Boolean = false,
250â†’    private val questionDelaySeconds: Int = 60,
251â†’    private val answerDelaySeconds: Int = 20
252â†’) {
253â†’
254â†’    val questionDelayMilliseconds = questionDelaySeconds * 1000L
255â†’    val answerDelayMilliseconds = answerDelaySeconds * 1000L
256â†’
257â†’    // a wait of zero means auto-advance is disabled
258â†’    val autoAdvanceAnswer; get() = answerDelaySeconds > 0
259â†’    val autoAdvanceQuestion; get() = questionDelaySeconds > 0
260â†’
261â†’    companion object {
262â†’        /**
263â†’         * Obtains the options for [AutomaticAnswer] in the deck config ("review" section)
264â†’         * @return null if the deck is dynamic (use global settings),
265â†’         * or if "useGeneralTimeoutSettings" is set
266â†’         */
267â†’        fun queryDeckSpecificOptions(
268â†’            action: AutomaticAnswerAction,
269â†’            col: Collection,
270â†’            selectedDid: DeckId
271â†’        ): AutomaticAnswerSettings? {
272â†’            // Dynamic don't have review options; attempt to get deck-specific auto-advance options
273â†’            // but be prepared to go with all default if it's a dynamic deck
274â†’            if (col.decks.isDyn(selectedDid)) {
275â†’                return null
276â†’            }
277â†’
278â†’            val revOptions = col.decks.confForDid(selectedDid).getJSONObject("rev")
279â†’
280â†’            if (revOptions.optBoolean("useGeneralTimeoutSettings", true)) {
281â†’                // we want to use the general settings, no need for per-deck settings
282â†’                return null
283â†’            }
284â†’
285â†’            val useTimer = revOptions.optBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
286â†’            val waitQuestionSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
287â†’            val waitAnswerSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
288â†’            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, useTimer, waitQuestionSecond, waitAnswerSecond)
289â†’        }
290â†’
291â†’        fun queryFromPreferences(preferences: SharedPreferences, action: AutomaticAnswerAction): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
292â†’            val prefUseTimer: Boolean = preferences.getBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
293â†’            val prefWaitQuestionSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
294â†’            val prefWaitAnswerSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
295â†’            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, prefUseTimer, prefWaitQuestionSecond, prefWaitAnswerSecond)
296â†’        }
297â†’
298â†’        fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
299â†’            // deck specific options take precedence over general (preference-based) options.
300â†’            // the action can only be set via preferences (but is stored in the collection).
301â†’            val action = getAction(col)
302â†’            return queryDeckSpecificOptions(action, col, col.decks.selected()) ?: queryFromPreferences(preferences, action)
303â†’        }
304â†’
305â†’        private fun getAction(col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerAction {
306â†’            return try {
307â†’                val value: Int = col.config.get(AutomaticAnswerAction.CONFIG_KEY) ?: return AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD
308â†’                AutomaticAnswerAction.fromPreferenceValue(value)
309â†’            } catch (e: Exception) {
310â†’                AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD
311â†’            }
312â†’        }
313â†’    }
314â†’}
315â†’
316â†’/**
317â†’ * Represents a value from [R.array.automatic_answer_values]/[R.array.automatic_answer_options]
318â†’ * Executed when answering a card (showing the question).
319â†’ */
320â†’enum class AutomaticAnswerAction(private val preferenceValue: Int) {
321â†’    /** Default: least invasive action */
322â†’    BURY_CARD(0),
323â†’    ANSWER_AGAIN(1),
324â†’    ANSWER_HARD(2),
325â†’    ANSWER_GOOD(3),
326â†’    ANSWER_EASY(4);
327â†’
328â†’    fun execute(reviewer: Reviewer) {
329â†’        val numberOfButtons = 4
330â†’        val actualAction = handleInvalidButtons(numberOfButtons)
331â†’        val action = actualAction.toCommand(numberOfButtons)
332â†’        Timber.i("Executing %s", action)
333â†’        reviewer.executeCommand(action)
334â†’    }
335â†’
336â†’    /** Handle **Hard/Easy** uf they don't appear */
337â†’    private fun handleInvalidButtons(numberOfButtons: Int): AutomaticAnswerAction {
338â†’        return when (this) {
339â†’            ANSWER_HARD -> if (AnswerButtons.canAnswerHard(numberOfButtons)) ANSWER_HARD else ANSWER_GOOD
340â†’            ANSWER_EASY -> if (AnswerButtons.canAnswerEasy(numberOfButtons)) ANSWER_EASY else ANSWER_GOOD
341â†’            // Again and Good always appear. So does Bury
342â†’            else -> this
343â†’        }
344â†’    }
345â†’
346â†’    /** Convert to a [ViewerCommand] */
347â†’    private fun toCommand(numberOfButtons: Int): ViewerCommand {
348â†’        return when (this) {
349â†’            BURY_CARD -> ViewerCommand.BURY_CARD
350â†’            ANSWER_AGAIN -> AGAIN.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
351â†’            ANSWER_HARD -> HARD.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
352â†’            ANSWER_GOOD -> GOOD.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
353â†’            ANSWER_EASY -> EASY.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
354â†’        }
355â†’    }
356â†’
357â†’    companion object {
358â†’        /**
359â†’         * An integer representing the action when Automatic Answer flips a card from answer to question
360â†’         *
361â†’         * 0 represents "bury", 1-4 represents the named buttons
362â†’         *
363â†’         * Although AnkiMobile and AnkiDroid have the feature, this config key is currently AnkiDroid only
364â†’         *
365â†’         * @see AutomaticAnswerAction
366â†’         */
367â†’        const val CONFIG_KEY = "automaticAnswerAction"
368â†’
369â†’        /** convert from [R.array.automatic_answer_values] ([R.array.automatic_answer_options]) to the enum */
370â†’        fun fromPreferenceValue(i: Int): AutomaticAnswerAction {
371â†’            return values().firstOrNull { it.preferenceValue == i } ?: BURY_CARD
372â†’        }
373â†’    }
374â†’}
375â†’

 26â†’package com.ichi2.libanki
 27â†’
 28â†’import anki.collection.OpChangesWithCount
 29â†’import anki.collection.OpChangesWithId
 30â†’import anki.decks.FilteredDeckForUpdate
 31â†’import com.google.protobuf.kotlin.toByteStringUtf8
 32â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.backend.BackendUtils
 33â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.utils.*
 34â†’import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 35â†’import com.ichi2.utils.jsonObjectIterable
 36â†’import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.RustCleanup
 37â†’import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.exceptions.BackendDeckIsFilteredException
 38â†’import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.exceptions.BackendNotFoundException
 39â†’import org.json.JSONArray
 40â†’import java.util.*
 41â†’
 42â†’data class DeckNameId(val name: String, val id: DeckId)
 43â†’
 44â†’// TODO: col was a weakref
 45â†’
 46â†’class Decks(private val col: Collection) {
 47â†’    /** "Add a deck with NAME. Reuse deck if already exists. Return id as int." */
 48â†’    fun id(name: String): DeckId {
 49â†’        val id = this.idForName(name)
 50â†’        if (id != null) {
 51â†’            return id
 52â†’        }
 53â†’        val deck = this.newDeckLegacy(false)
 54â†’        deck.name = name
 55â†’        addDeckLegacy(deck)
 56â†’        return deck.id
 57â†’    }
 58â†’
 59â†’    private fun addDeckLegacy(deck: Deck): OpChangesWithId {
 60â†’        val changes = col.backend.addDeckLegacy(
 61â†’            json = BackendUtils.to_json_bytes(deck)
 62â†’        )
 63â†’        deck.id = changes.id
 64â†’        return changes
 65â†’    }
 66â†’
 67â†’    fun removeDecks(deckIds: Iterable<Long>): OpChangesWithCount {
 68â†’        return col.backend.removeDecks(dids = deckIds)
 69â†’    }
 70â†’
 71â†’    /** A sorted sequence of deck names and IDs. */
 72â†’    fun allNamesAndIds(
 73â†’        skipEmptyDefault: Boolean = false,
 74â†’        includeFiltered: Boolean = true
 75â†’    ): List<DeckNameId> {
 76â†’        return col.backend.getDeckNames(skipEmptyDefault = skipEmptyDefault, includeFiltered = includeFiltered).map { entry ->
 77â†’            DeckNameId(entry.name, entry.id)
 78â†’        }
 79â†’    }
 80â†’
 81â†’    fun idForName(name: String): DeckId? {
 82â†’        return try {
 83â†’            col.backend.getDeckIdByName(name)
 84â†’        } catch (ex: BackendNotFoundException) {
 85â†’            null
 86â†’        }
 87â†’    }
 88â†’
 89â†’    fun get(did: DeckId): Deck? {
 90â†’        return try {
 91â†’            Deck(BackendUtils.from_json_bytes(col.backend.getDeckLegacy(did)))
 92â†’        } catch (ex: BackendNotFoundException) {
 93â†’            null
 94â†’        }
 95â†’    }
 96â†’
 97â†’    private fun newDeckLegacy(filtered: Boolean): Deck {
 98â†’        val deck = BackendUtils.from_json_bytes(col.backend.newDeckLegacy(filtered))
 99â†’        return Deck(
100â†’            if (filtered) {
101â†’                // until migrating to the dedicated method for creating filtered decks,
102â†’                // we need to ensure the default config matches legacy expectations
103â†’                val terms = deck.getJSONArray("terms").getJSONArray(0)
104â†’                terms.put(0, "")
105â†’                terms.put(2, 0)
106â†’                deck.put("terms", JSONArray(listOf(terms)))
107â†’                deck.put("browserCollapsed", false)
108â†’                deck.put("collapsed", false)
109â†’                deck
110â†’            } else {
111â†’                deck
112â†’            }
113â†’        )
114â†’    }
115â†’
116â†’    fun collapse(did: DeckId) {
117â†’        val deck = this.get(did) ?: return
118â†’        deck.collapsed = !deck.collapsed
119â†’        this.save(deck)
120â†’    }
121â†’
122â†’    fun count(): Int {
123â†’        return len(this.allNamesAndIds())
124â†’    }
125â†’
126â†’    /** Get deck with NAME, ignoring case. */
127â†’    fun byName(name: String): Deck? {
128â†’        val id = this.idForName(name)
129â†’        if (id != null) {
130â†’            return get(id)
131â†’        }
132â†’        return null
133â†’    }
134â†’
135â†’    /**
136â†’     * Add or update an existing deck. Used for syncing and merging.
137â†’     * @throws BackendDeckIsFilteredException
138â†’     */
139â†’    fun save(g: Deck) {
140â†’        g.id = col.backend.addOrUpdateDeckLegacy(
141â†’            BackendUtils.toByteString(g),
142â†’            preserveUsnAndMtime = false
143â†’        )
144â†’    }
145â†’
146â†’    /** Rename deck prefix to NAME if not exists. Updates children. */
147â†’    fun rename(g: Deck, newName: String) {
148â†’        g.name = newName
149â†’        this.save(g)
150â†’    }
151â†’
152â†’    /* Deck configurations */
153â†’
154â†’    /** A list of all deck config. */
155â†’    fun allConfig(): List<DeckConfig> {
156â†’        return BackendUtils.jsonToArray(col.backend.allDeckConfigLegacy())
157â†’            .jsonObjectIterable()
158â†’            .map { obj -> DeckConfig(obj) }
159â†’            .toList()
160â†’    }
161â†’
162â†’    /** Falls back on default config if deck or config missing */
163â†’    fun confForDid(did: DeckId): DeckConfig {
164â†’        val conf = get(did)?.conf ?: 1
165â†’        return DeckConfig(BackendUtils.from_json_bytes(col.backend.getDeckConfigLegacy(conf)))
166â†’    }
167â†’
168â†’    fun save(g: DeckConfig) {
169â†’        g.id = col.backend.addOrUpdateDeckConfigLegacy(g.toString().toByteStringUtf8())
170â†’    }
171â†’
172â†’    private fun addConfig(
173â†’        name: String
174â†’    ): DeckConfig {
175â†’        val conf = DeckConfig(newDeckConfigLegacy())
176â†’        conf.name = name
177â†’        this.save(conf)
178â†’        return conf
179â†’    }
180â†’
181â†’    private fun newDeckConfigLegacy(): DeckConfig {
182â†’        return DeckConfig(BackendUtils.from_json_bytes(col.backend.newDeckConfigLegacy()))
183â†’    }
184â†’
185â†’    fun setConf(grp: Deck, id: DeckConfigId) {
186â†’        grp.conf = id
187â†’        this.save(grp)
188â†’    }
189â†’
190â†’    /* Reverts to default if provided id missing */
191â†’    fun getConf(confId: DeckConfigId): DeckConfig =
192â†’        DeckConfig(BackendUtils.from_json_bytes(col.backend.getDeckConfigLegacy(confId)))
193â†’
194â†’    fun confId(name: String): Long {
195â†’        return addConfig(name).id
196â†’    }
197â†’
198â†’    /* Deck selection */
199â†’
200â†’    /** The currently active dids. */
201â†’    @RustCleanup("Probably better as a queue")
202â†’    fun active(): LinkedList<DeckId> {
203â†’        val activeDecks = col.config.get<List<DeckId>>(ACTIVE_DECKS) ?: listOf()
204â†’        val result = LinkedList<Long>()
205â†’        result.addAll(activeDecks.asIterable())
206â†’        return result
207â†’    }
208â†’
209â†’    /** The currently selected did. */
210â†’    fun selected(): DeckId {
211â†’        return this.col.backend.getCurrentDeck().id
212â†’    }
213â†’
214â†’    fun current(): Deck {
215â†’        return this.get(this.selected()) ?: this.get(1)!!
216â†’    }
217â†’
218â†’    /** Select a new branch. */
219â†’    fun select(did: DeckId) {
220â†’        col.backend.setCurrentDeck(did)
221â†’        val selectedDeckName = name(did)
222â†’        val childrenDids =
223â†’            allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = false)
224â†’                .filter { it.name.startsWith("$selectedDeckName::") }
225â†’                .map { it.id }
226â†’        col.config.set(ACTIVE_DECKS, listOf(did) + childrenDids)
227â†’    }
228â†’
229â†’    /*
230â†’     Dynamic decks
231â†’     */
232â†’
233â†’    /** Return a new dynamic deck and set it as the current deck. */
234â†’    fun newDyn(name: String): DeckId {
235â†’        val deck = this.newDeckLegacy(true)
236â†’        deck.name = name
237â†’        addDeckLegacy(deck)
238â†’        this.select(deck.id)
239â†’        return deck.id
240â†’    }
241â†’
242â†’    fun isDyn(did: DeckId): Boolean {
243â†’        return this.get(did)?.isFiltered == true
244â†’    }
245â†’
246â†’    fun name(did: DeckId): String = get(did)?.name ?: "[no deck]"
247â†’
248â†’    /*
249â†’     * ***********************************************************
250â†’     * The methods below are not in LibAnki.
251â†’     * ***********************************************************
252â†’     */
253â†’    @KotlinCleanup("convert to extension method (possibly in servicelayer)")
254â†’    fun getActualDescription(): String = current().optString("desc", "")
255â†’
256â†’    /** @return the fully qualified name of the subdeck, or null if unavailable */
257â†’    fun getSubdeckName(did: DeckId, subdeckName: String?): String? {
258â†’        if (subdeckName.isNullOrEmpty()) {
259â†’            return null
260â†’        }
261â†’        val newName = subdeckName.replace("\"".toRegex(), "")
262â†’        if (newName.isEmpty()) {
263â†’            return null
264â†’        }
265â†’        val deck = get(did) ?: return null
266â†’        return deck.getString("name") + DECK_SEPARATOR + subdeckName
267â†’    }
268â†’
269â†’    /*
270â†’     * Not in libAnki
271â†’     */
272â†’
273â†’    companion object {
274â†’        /* Parents/children */
275â†’
276â†’        fun path(name: String): List<String> {
277â†’            return name.split("::")
278â†’        }
279â†’
280â†’        fun basename(name: String): String {
281â†’            return path(name).last()
282â†’        }
283â†’
284â†’        /** Invalid id, represents an id on an unfound deck  */
285â†’        const val NOT_FOUND_DECK_ID = -1L
286â†’
287â†’        /** Configuration saving the current deck  */
288â†’        const val CURRENT_DECK = "curDeck"
289â†’
290â†’        /** Configuration saving the set of active decks (i.e. current decks and its descendants)  */
291â†’        const val ACTIVE_DECKS = "activeDecks"
292â†’
293â†’        // not in libAnki
294â†’        const val DECK_SEPARATOR = "::"
295â†’
296â†’    /*
297â†’    * ***********************************************************
298â†’    * The methods below are not in LibAnki.
299â†’    * ***********************************************************
300â†’    */
301â†’        fun isValidDeckName(deckName: String): Boolean = deckName.trim { it <= ' ' }.isNotEmpty()
302â†’    }
303â†’}
304â†’
305â†’// These take and return bytes that the frontend TypeScript code will encode/decode.
306â†’fun Collection.getDeckNamesRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
307â†’    return backend.getDeckNamesRaw(input)
308â†’}
309â†’
310â†’/**
311â†’ * Gets the filtered deck with given [did]
312â†’ * or creates a new one if [did] = 0
313â†’ */
314â†’fun Collection.getOrCreateFilteredDeck(did: DeckId): FilteredDeckForUpdate {
315â†’    return backend.getOrCreateFilteredDeck(did = did)
316â†’}
317â†’

14â†’package com.ichi2.anki.pages
15â†’
16â†’import android.content.Context
17â†’import android.content.Intent
18â†’import android.os.Bundle
19â†’import android.webkit.WebView
20â†’import androidx.activity.OnBackPressedCallback
21â†’import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
22â†’import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager
23â†’import com.ichi2.anki.R
24â†’import com.ichi2.anki.SingleFragmentActivity
25â†’import com.ichi2.anki.hideShowButtonCss
26â†’
27â†’class AnkiPackageImporterFragment : PageFragment() {
28â†’    override val title: String
29â†’        get() = resources.getString(R.string.menu_import)
30â†’    override val pageName: String
31â†’        get() = "import-page"
32â†’    override lateinit var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
33â†’    override var webChromeClient: PageChromeClient = PageChromeClient()
34â†’    private lateinit var backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
35â†’
36â†’    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
37â†’        // the back callback is only enabled when import is running and showing progress
38â†’        backCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
39â†’            override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
40â†’                CollectionManager.getBackend().setWantsAbort()
41â†’                // once triggered the callback is not needed as the import process can't be resumed
42â†’                remove()
43â†’            }
44â†’        }
45â†’        val path = arguments?.getString(ARG_FILE_PATH)
46â†’            ?: throw IllegalStateException("No path provided for apkg package to import")
47â†’        webViewClient = AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(path, backCallback)
48â†’        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
49â†’        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, backCallback)
50â†’    }
51â†’
52â†’    class AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(
53â†’        private val path: String,
54â†’        private val backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
55â†’    ) : PageWebViewClient() {
56â†’        /**
57â†’         * Ideally, to handle the state of the back callback, we would just need to check for
58â†’         * `/latestProgress` calls followed by one `/importDone` call. However there are some extra
59â†’         * calls to `/latestProgress` AFTER `/importDone` and this property keeps track of this.
60â†’         */
61â†’        private var isDone = false
62â†’
63â†’        override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
64â†’            val params = """{ type: "json_file", path: "$path"}"""
65â†’            // https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/main/ts/import-page/index.ts
66â†’            view!!.evaluateJavascript("anki.setupImportPage($params);$hideShowButtonCss;") {
67â†’                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
68â†’            }
69â†’        }
70â†’
71â†’        override fun onLoadResource(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
72â†’            super.onLoadResource(view, url)
73â†’            backCallback.isEnabled = when {
74â†’                url == null -> false
75â†’                url.endsWith("latestProgress") && !isDone -> true
76â†’                url.endsWith("importDone") -> {
77â†’                    isDone = true // import was done so disable any back callback changes after this call
78â†’                    false
79â†’                }
80â†’                else -> false
81â†’            }
82â†’        }
83â†’    }
84â†’
85â†’    companion object {
86â†’        private const val ARG_FILE_PATH = "arg_file_path"
87â†’
88â†’        fun getIntent(context: Context, filePath: String): Intent {
89â†’            val args = bundleOf(ARG_FILE_PATH to filePath)
90â†’            return SingleFragmentActivity.getIntent(context, AnkiPackageImporterFragment::class, args)
91â†’        }
92â†’    }
93â†’}
94â†’

16â†’package com.ichi2.anki.pages
17â†’
18â†’import android.os.Bundle
19â†’import android.view.LayoutInflater
20â†’import android.view.View
21â†’import android.view.ViewGroup
22â†’import android.webkit.WebView
23â†’import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
24â†’import com.google.android.material.appbar.MaterialToolbar
25â†’import com.ichi2.anki.R
26â†’import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
27â†’import timber.log.Timber
28â†’
29â†’/**
30â†’ * Base class for displaying Anki HTML pages
31â†’ */
32â†’@Suppress("LeakingThis")
33â†’abstract class PageFragment : Fragment(), PostRequestHandler {
34â†’    abstract val title: String
35â†’    abstract val pageName: String
36â†’    abstract var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
37â†’    abstract var webChromeClient: PageChromeClient
38â†’
39â†’    lateinit var webView: WebView
40â†’    private val server = AnkiServer(this).also { it.start() }
41â†’
42â†’    override fun onCreateView(
43â†’        inflater: LayoutInflater,
44â†’        container: ViewGroup?,
45â†’        savedInstanceState: Bundle?
46â†’    ): View? {
47â†’        val view = inflater.inflate(R.layout.page_fragment, container, false)
48â†’
49â†’        webView = view.findViewById<WebView>(R.id.webview).apply {
50â†’            with(settings) {
51â†’                javaScriptEnabled = true
52â†’                displayZoomControls = false
53â†’                builtInZoomControls = true
54â†’                setSupportZoom(true)
55â†’            }
56â†’            webViewClient = this@PageFragment.webViewClient
57â†’            webChromeClient = this@PageFragment.webChromeClient
58â†’        }
59â†’        val nightMode = if (Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode) "#night" else ""
60â†’        val url = server.baseUrl() + "$pageName.html$nightMode"
61â†’
62â†’        Timber.i("Loading $url")
63â†’        webView.loadUrl(url)
64â†’
65â†’        view.findViewById<MaterialToolbar>(R.id.toolbar).apply {
66â†’            title = this@PageFragment.title
67â†’            setNavigationOnClickListener {
68â†’                requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
69â†’            }
70â†’        }
71â†’        return view
72â†’    }
73â†’
74â†’    override suspend fun handlePostRequest(uri: String, bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
75â†’        val methodName = if (uri.startsWith(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX)) {
76â†’            uri.substring(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX.length)
77â†’        } else {
78â†’            throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $uri")
79â†’        }
80â†’        return requireActivity().handleUiPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
81â†’            ?: handleCollectionPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
82â†’            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled method: $methodName")
83â†’    }
84â†’}
85â†’

16â†’package com.ichi2.anki
17â†’
18â†’import android.content.Context
19â†’import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
20â†’import androidx.webkit.WebViewAssetLoader
21â†’import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.guessMimeType
22â†’import timber.log.Timber
23â†’import java.io.File
24â†’import java.io.FileInputStream
25â†’
26â†’fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
27â†’    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
28â†’    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
29â†’        .setHttpAllowed(true)
30â†’        .setDomain(domain)
31â†’        .addPathHandler("/web/") { path: String ->
32â†’            val inputStream = this.javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream("web/$path")
33â†’            WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
34â†’        }
35â†’        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
36â†’            try {
37â†’                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
38â†’                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
39â†’                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
40â†’            } catch (e: Exception) {
41â†’                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
42â†’                null
43â†’            }
44â†’        }
45â†’        .build()
46â†’}
47â†’

17â†’package com.ichi2.anki
18â†’
19â†’import android.content.Intent
20â†’import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentActivity
21â†’import anki.collection.OpChangesOnly
22â†’import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol
23â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.buildSearchString
24â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.importAnkiPackageRaw
25â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.importCsvRaw
26â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.undoableOp
27â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
28â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
29â†’
30â†’suspend fun importJsonFileRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
31â†’    return withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
32â†’        val output = withCol { this.importAnkiPackageRaw(input) }
33â†’        val changes = OpChangesOnly.parseFrom(output)
34â†’        undoableOp { changes }
35â†’        output
36â†’    }
37â†’}
38â†’
39â†’suspend fun importCsvRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
40â†’    return withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
41â†’        val output = withCol { importCsvRaw(input) }
42â†’        val changes = OpChangesOnly.parseFrom(output)
43â†’        undoableOp { changes }
44â†’        output
45â†’    }
46â†’}
47â†’
48â†’/**
49â†’ * Css to hide the "Show" button from the final backend import page. As the user could import a lot
50â†’ * of notes, on pressing "Show" the native CardBrowser would be called with a search query
51â†’ * comprising of all the notes ids. This would result in a crash or very slow behavior in the
52â†’ * CardBrowser.
53â†’ *
54â†’ * NOTE: this should be used only with [android.webkit.WebView.evaluateJavascript].
55â†’ */
56â†’val hideShowButtonCss = """
57â†’    javascript:(
58â†’        function() {
59â†’            var hideShowButtonStyle = '.desktop-only { display: none !important; }';
60â†’            var newStyle = document.createElement('style');                    
61â†’            newStyle.appendChild(document.createTextNode(hideShowButtonStyle));
62â†’            document.head.appendChild(newStyle);       
63â†’        }
64â†’    )()
65â†’""".trimIndent()
66â†’
67â†’/**
68â†’ * Calls the native [CardBrowser] to display the results of the search query constructed from the
69â†’ * input. This method will always return the received input.
70â†’ */
71â†’suspend fun FragmentActivity.searchInBrowser(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
72â†’    val searchString = withCol { buildSearchString(input) }
73â†’    val starterIntent = Intent(this, CardBrowser::class.java).apply {
74â†’        putExtra("search_query", searchString)
75â†’        putExtra("all_decks", true)
76â†’    }
77â†’    startActivity(starterIntent)
78â†’    return input
79â†’}

 105â†’open class Reviewer :
 106â†’    AbstractFlashcardViewer(),
 107â†’    ReviewerUi,
 108â†’    PostRequestHandler {
 109â†’    private var queueState: CurrentQueueState? = null
 110â†’    private val customSchedulingKey = TimeManager.time.intTimeMS().toString()

 120â†’        get() = "$LOCALHOST:${server.listeningPort}"
 121â†’
 122â†’    @VisibleForTesting
 123â†’    val jsApi by lazy { AnkiDroidJsAPI(this) }
 124â†’
 125â†’    // A flag that determines if the SchedulingStates in CurrentQueueState are
 126â†’    // safe to persist in the database when answering a card. This is used to
 127â†’    // ensure that the custom JS scheduler has persisted its SchedulingStates
 128â†’    // back to the Reviewer before we save it to the database. If the custom
 129â†’    // scheduler has not been configured, then it is safe to immediately set
 130â†’    // this to true
 131â†’    //
 132â†’    // This flag should be set to false when we show the front of the card
 133â†’    // and only set to true once we know the custom scheduler has finished its
 134â†’    // execution, or set to true immediately if the custom scheduler has not
 135â†’    // been configured
 136â†’    private var statesMutated = false
 137â†’
 138â†’    // TODO: Consider extracting to ViewModel
 139â†’    // Card counts
 140â†’    private var mNewCount: SpannableString? = null
 141â†’    private var mLrnCount: SpannableString? = null
 142â†’    private var mRevCount: SpannableString? = null
 143â†’    private lateinit var mTextBarNew: TextView
 144â†’    private lateinit var mTextBarLearn: TextView
 145â†’    private lateinit var mTextBarReview: TextView
 146â†’    private lateinit var answerTimer: AnswerTimer
 147â†’    private var mPrefHideDueCount = false
 148â†’
 149â†’    // Whiteboard
 150â†’    var prefWhiteboard = false
 151â†’
 152â†’    @get:CheckResult
 153â†’    @get:VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
 154â†’    var whiteboard: Whiteboard? = null
 155â†’        protected set

 157â†’    // Record Audio

 161â†’    private lateinit var micToolBarLayer: LinearLayout

 166â†’    /** Handle Mark/Flag state of cards  */
 167â†’    @VisibleForTesting
 168â†’    internal var mCardMarker: CardMarker? = null
 169â†’
 170â†’    // Preferences from the collection

 172â†’    private var stopTimerOnAnswer = false
 173â†’    private val mActionButtons = ActionButtons()
 174â†’    private lateinit var mToolbar: Toolbar

 185â†’    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {

 244â†’    override fun shouldDisplayMark(): Boolean {
 245â†’        val markValue = super.shouldDisplayMark()
 246â†’        if (!markValue) {

 250â†’        // If we don't know: assume it's not shown

 255â†’    protected open fun onMark(card: Card?) {
 256â†’        if (card == null) {
 257â†’            return
 258â†’        }
 259â†’        launchCatchingTask {
 260â†’            toggleMark(card.note(), handler = this@Reviewer)
 261â†’            refreshActionBar()

 264â†’    }

 273â†’    protected open fun onFlag(card: Card?, flag: Flag) {
 274â†’        if (card == null) {
 275â†’            return
 276â†’        }
 277â†’        launchCatchingTask {
 278â†’            card.setUserFlag(flag.code)
 279â†’            withCol {

 281â†’            }

 287â†’    private fun onFlagChanged() {

 294â†’    private fun selectDeckFromExtra() {
 295â†’        val extras = intent.extras
 296â†’        if (extras == null || !extras.containsKey("deckId")) {
 297â†’            // deckId is not set, load default
 298â†’            return
 299â†’        }
 300â†’        val did = extras.getLong("deckId", Long.MIN_VALUE)
 301â†’        Timber.d("selectDeckFromExtra() with deckId = %d", did)
 302â†’
 303â†’        // deckId does not exist, load default
 304â†’        if (getColUnsafe.decks.get(did) == null) {
 305â†’            Timber.w("selectDeckFromExtra() deckId '%d' doesn't exist", did)
 306â†’            return
 307â†’        }
 308â†’        // Select the deck
 309â†’        getColUnsafe.decks.select(did)
 310â†’    }

 312â†’    override fun getContentViewAttr(fullscreenMode: FullScreenMode): Int {
 313â†’        return when (fullscreenMode) {

 315â†’            FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE -> R.layout.reviewer_fullscreen_noanswers
 316â†’            else -> R.layout.reviewer

 324â†’    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
 325â†’        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
 326â†’        if (Intent.ACTION_VIEW == intent.action) {
 327â†’            Timber.d("onCreate() :: received Intent with action = %s", intent.action)
 328â†’            selectDeckFromExtra()
 329â†’        }
 330â†’        // Load the first card and start reviewing. Uses the answer card
 331â†’        // task to load a card, but since we send null
 332â†’        // as the card to answer, no card will be answered.
 333â†’        prefWhiteboard = MetaDB.getWhiteboardState(this, parentDid)
 334â†’        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 335â†’            // DEFECT: Slight inefficiency here, as we set the database using these methods
 336â†’            val whiteboardVisibility = MetaDB.getWhiteboardVisibility(this, parentDid)
 337â†’            setWhiteboardEnabledState(true)
 338â†’            setWhiteboardVisibility(whiteboardVisibility)
 339â†’            toggleStylus = MetaDB.getWhiteboardStylusState(this, parentDid)
 340â†’            whiteboard!!.toggleStylus = toggleStylus

 342â†’        launchCatchingTask {
 343â†’            withCol { startTimebox() }
 344â†’            updateCardAndRedraw()

 346â†’        disableDrawerSwipeOnConflicts()
 347â†’
 348â†’        // Set full screen/immersive mode if needed
 349â†’        if (mPrefFullscreenReview) {

 351â†’        }
 352â†’        setRenderWorkaround(this)
 353â†’    }

 936â†’    override fun displayAnswerBottomBar() {
 937â†’        super.displayAnswerBottomBar()
 938â†’        mOnboarding.onAnswerShown()
 939â†’        // Set correct label and background resource for each button
 940â†’        // Note that it's necessary to set the resource dynamically as the ease2 / ease3 buttons
 941â†’        // (which libanki expects ease to be 2 and 3) can either be hard, good, or easy - depending on num buttons shown
 942â†’        val background = getBackgroundColors(this)
 943â†’        val textColor = getTextColors(this)
 944â†’        easeButton1!!.setVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
 945â†’        easeButton1!!.setColor(background[0])
 946â†’        easeButton4!!.setColor(background[3])
 947â†’        // Ease 2 is "hard"
 948â†’        easeButton2!!.setup(background[1], textColor[1], R.string.ease_button_hard)
 949â†’        easeButton2!!.requestFocus()
 950â†’        // Ease 3 is good
 951â†’        easeButton3!!.setup(background[2], textColor[2], R.string.ease_button_good)
 952â†’        easeButton4!!.setVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
 953â†’        easeButton3!!.requestFocus()
 954â†’
 955â†’        // Show next review time
 956â†’        if (shouldShowNextReviewTime()) {
 957â†’            val state = queueState!!
 958â†’            launchCatchingTask {
 959â†’                val labels = withCol { sched.describeNextStates(state.states) }
 960â†’                easeButton1!!.nextTime = labels[0]
 961â†’                easeButton2!!.nextTime = labels[1]
 962â†’                easeButton3!!.nextTime = labels[2]
 963â†’                easeButton4!!.nextTime = labels[3]
 964â†’            }
 965â†’        }
 966â†’    }

 55â†’        // The version number is of the form:
 56â†’        // <major>.<minor>.<maintenance>[dev|alpha<build>|beta<build>|]
 57â†’        // The <build> is only present for alpha and beta releases (e.g., 2.0.4alpha2 or 2.0.4beta4), developer builds do
 58â†’        // not have a build number (e.g., 2.0.4dev) and official releases only have three components (e.g., 2.0.4).
 59â†’        //
 60â†’        // The version code is derived from the version name as follows:
 61â†’        // AbbCCtDD
 62â†’        // A: 1-digit decimal number representing the major version
 63â†’        // bb: 2-digit decimal number representing the minor version
 64â†’        // CC: 2-digit decimal number representing the maintenance version
 65â†’        // t: 1-digit decimal number representing the type of the build
 66â†’        // 0: developer build
 67â†’        // 1: alpha release
 68â†’        // 2: beta release
 69â†’        // 3: public release
 70â†’        // DD: 2-digit decimal number representing the build
 71â†’        // 00 for internal builds and public releases
 72â†’        // alpha/beta build number for alpha/beta releases
 73â†’        //
 74â†’        // This ensures the correct ordering between the various types of releases (dev < alpha < beta < release) which is
 75â†’        // needed for upgrades to be offered correctly.

153â†’    /**
154â†’     * Product Flavors are used for Amazon App Store and Google Play Store.
155â†’     * This is because we cannot use Camera Permissions in Amazon App Store (for FireTv etc...)
156â†’     * Therefore, different AndroidManifest for Camera Permissions is used in Amazon flavor.
157â†’     *
158â†’     * This flavor block must stay in sync with the same block in testlib/build.gradle.kts
159â†’     */
160â†’    flavorDimensions += "appStore"
161â†’    productFlavors {
162â†’        create('play') {
163â†’            dimension "appStore"
164â†’        }
165â†’        create('amazon') {
166â†’            dimension "appStore"
167â†’        }
168â†’        // A 'full' build has no restrictions on storage/camera. Distributed on GitHub/F-Droid
169â†’        create('full') {
170â†’            dimension "appStore"
171â†’        }
172â†’    }
173â†’
174â†’    /**
175â†’     * Set this to true to create five separate APKs instead of one:
176â†’     *   - 2 APKs that only work on ARM/ARM64 devices
177â†’     *   - 2 APKs that only works on x86/x86_64 devices
178â†’     *   - a universal APK that works on all devices
179â†’     * The advantage is the size of most APKs is reduced by about 2.5MB.
180â†’     * Upload all the APKs to the Play Store and people will download
181â†’     * the correct one based on the CPU architecture of their device.
182â†’     */
183â†’    def enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture = true
184â†’
185â†’    splits {
186â†’        abi {
187â†’            reset()
188â†’            enable enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture
189â†’            //universalApk enableUniversalApk  // set in debug + release config blocks above
190â†’            include "armeabi-v7a", "x86", "arm64-v8a", "x86_64"
191â†’        }
192â†’    }
193â†’    // applicationVariants are e.g. debug, release
194â†’    applicationVariants.configureEach { variant ->
195â†’        // We want the same version stream for all ABIs in debug but for release we can split them
196â†’        if (variant.buildType.name == 'release') {
197â†’            variant.outputs.configureEach { output ->
198â†’
199â†’                // For each separate APK per architecture, set a unique version code as described here:
200â†’                // https://developer.android.com/studio/build/configure-apk-splits.html
201â†’                def versionCodes = ["armeabi-v7a": 1, "x86": 2, "arm64-v8a": 3, "x86_64": 4]
202â†’                def outputFile = output.outputFile
203â†’                if (outputFile != null && outputFile.name.endsWith('.apk')) {
204â†’                    def abi = output.getFilter("ABI")
205â†’                    if (abi != null) {  // null for the universal-debug, universal-release variants
206â†’                        //  From: https://developer.android.com/studio/publish/versioning#appversioning
207â†’                        //  "Warning: The greatest value Google Play allows for versionCode is 2100000000"
208â†’                        //  AnkiDroid versionCodes have a budget 8 digits (through AnkiDroid 9)
209â†’                        //  This style does ABI version code ranges with the 9th digit as 0-4.
210â†’                        //  This consumes ~20% of the version range space, w/50 years of versioning at our major-version pace
211â†’                        output.versionCodeOverride =
212â†’                                // ex:  321200106 = 3 * 100000000 + 21200106
213â†’                                versionCodes.get(abi) * 100000000 + defaultConfig.versionCode
214â†’                    }
215â†’                }
216â†’            }
217â†’        }

content.text:
The AnkiDroid backend has been updated to version 23.12, including necessary code changes to support the updated FSRS algorithm and other backend improvements.

### Changes implemented:

1.  **Backend Version Update:**
    *   Updated `ankidroid_backend_version` in the root `build.gradle` to `0.1.34-anki23.12`.
    *   Updated `Collection.exportAnkiPackage` in `BackendImportExport.kt` to use the new `exportAnkiPackageOptions` API. As recommended in the issue discussion, this hard-codes `withDeckConfigs = true` as a temporary workaround until the full import/export options UI is implemented.

2.  **Filtered Deck Preview Delays:**
    *   Modified `FilteredDeckOptions.kt` to support the new `previewAgainSecs`, `previewHardSecs`, and `previewGoodSecs` settings for filtered decks.
    *   Added logic to load these values from the deck's configuration and save them back when modified.
    *   Implemented a UI listener to enable/disable the "Preview Delays" category based on the "Reschedule cards based on my answers in this deck" setting, as these delays only apply when rescheduling is disabled.

3.  **Auto-Advance Settings:**
    *   Improved the auto-advance feature to correctly use the deck-specific settings of the current card's deck, rather than just the globally selected deck.
    *   Updated `AutomaticAnswer` and its settings to allow refreshing configuration when a card from a different deck is loaded.
    *   Modified `AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` to trigger this update whenever a new card is displayed.

These changes ensure compatibility with Anki 23.12's scheduler and provide users with the new configuration options introduced in this version.

đź¤– Generated with [Firebender](https://firebender.com)

Co-Authored-By: Firebender <help@firebender.com>

 23â†’/**
 24â†’ * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 25â†’ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 26â†’ * and this routine will return false.
 27â†’ *
 28â†’ * There must not be an active transaction.
 29â†’ *
 30â†’ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 31â†’ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 32â†’ * to check for success.
 33â†’ *
 34â†’ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 35â†’ *
 36â†’ */
 37â†’fun Collection.createBackup(
 38â†’    backupFolder: String,
 39â†’    force: Boolean,
 40â†’    waitForCompletion: Boolean
 41â†’): Boolean {
 42â†’    return backend.createBackup(
 43â†’        backupFolder = backupFolder,
 44â†’        force = force,
 45â†’        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion
 46â†’    )
 47â†’}

 49â†’/**
 50â†’ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 51â†’ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 52â†’ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 53â†’ */
 54â†’fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
 55â†’    backend.awaitBackupCompletion()

 31â†’/**
 32â†’ * AnkiDroid contains a setting: "Automatic display answer" which displays the
 33â†’ * question/answer automatically after a set period of time.
 34â†’ *
 35â†’ * ## Settings
 36â†’ * See: [AutomaticAnswerSettings]
 37â†’ *
 38â†’ * ## Implementation
 39â†’ *
 40â†’ * If "Enabled" is disabled, then nothing happens
 41â†’ * If Time to show Answer is 0, the automatic flipping code is disabled
 42â†’ * If Time to show next question is 0, the automatic answering code is disabled
 43â†’ *
 44â†’ * When a card is displayed (Front)
 45â†’ *
 46â†’ * If it has neither TTS nor audio, schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 47â†’ * If it has audio, calculate the time for the audio to play
 48â†’ *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" + "audio runtime" seconds
 49â†’ * If it has TTS, add a handler to the completion of TTS
 50â†’ *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 51â†’ *
 52â†’ * Do the same for the Back, but use the "Time to show next question" setting.
 53â†’ *
 54â†’ * If the "next question" is shown, "Again" is pressed.
 55â†’ *
 56â†’ * The implementation originally had multiple bugs regarding double presses of the answer buttons.
 57â†’ * This is because the user can press show the card at the same instant that the automatic code executes
 58â†’ *
 59â†’ * This has been worked around via the calls: [onSelectEase] and [onShowAnswer].
 60â†’ * Both of which clear the task queue for the question ([onSelectEase] or vice-versa)
 61â†’ */
 62â†’class AutomaticAnswer(
 63â†’    target: AutomaticallyAnswered,
 64â†’    @VisibleForTesting val settings: AutomaticAnswerSettings
 65â†’) {
 66â†’
 67â†’    /** Whether any tasks should be executed/scheduled.
 68â†’     *
 69â†’     * Ensures that auto answer does not occur if the reviewer is minimised
 70â†’     */
 71â†’    var isDisabled: Boolean = false
 72â†’        private set
 73â†’
 74â†’    /**
 75â†’     * Whether the sounds for the current side of the card have been played
 76â†’     * So [scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer] and [scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion] do nothing
 77â†’     * if called multiple times on the same side of the card
 78â†’     */
 79â†’    private var hasPlayedSounds: Boolean = false
 80â†’
 81â†’    private val showAnswerTask = Runnable {
 82â†’        if (isDisabled) {
 83â†’            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
 84â†’            return@Runnable
 85â†’        }
 86â†’        target.automaticShowAnswer()
 87â†’    }
 88â†’    private val showQuestionTask = Runnable {
 89â†’        if (isDisabled) {
 90â†’            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
 91â†’            return@Runnable
 92â†’        }
 93â†’        target.automaticShowQuestion(settings.answerAction)
 94â†’    }
 95â†’
 96â†’    /**
 97â†’     * Handler for the delay in auto showing question and/or answer
 98â†’     * One toggle for both question and answer, could set longer delay for auto next question
 99â†’     */
100â†’    @VisibleForTesting
101â†’    val timeoutHandler = HandlerUtils.newHandler()
102â†’
103â†’    @VisibleForTesting
104â†’    fun delayedShowQuestion(delay: Long) {
105â†’        if (isDisabled) {
106â†’            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
107â†’            return
108â†’        }
109â†’        Timber.i("Automatically showing question in %dms", delay)
110â†’        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showQuestionTask, delay)
111â†’    }
112â†’
113â†’    @VisibleForTesting
114â†’    fun delayedShowAnswer(delay: Long) {

231â†’/**
232â†’ * ### Reviewing - Automatic display answer
233â†’ *
234â†’ * * Enabled
235â†’ * * Time to show answer (20s)
236â†’ * * Time to show next question (60s)
237â†’ * * Automatic action: the action to take when reviewing
238â†’ *
239â†’ * ### Deck Options - Reviews - Automatic Display Answer
240â†’ *
241â†’ * These settings also exist in an options group
242â†’ * Although they're under "reviews", it counts for all cards
243â†’ *
244â†’ * * Use General settings (using the settings above - enabled by default)
245â†’ * * Enabled, etc...
246â†’ */
247â†’class AutomaticAnswerSettings(
248â†’    val answerAction: AutomaticAnswerAction = AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD,
249â†’    @get:JvmName("useTimer") val useTimer: Boolean = false,
250â†’    private val questionDelaySeconds: Int = 60,
251â†’    private val answerDelaySeconds: Int = 20
252â†’) {
253â†’
254â†’    val questionDelayMilliseconds = questionDelaySeconds * 1000L
255â†’    val answerDelayMilliseconds = answerDelaySeconds * 1000L
256â†’
257â†’    // a wait of zero means auto-advance is disabled
258â†’    val autoAdvanceAnswer; get() = answerDelaySeconds > 0
259â†’    val autoAdvanceQuestion; get() = questionDelaySeconds > 0
260â†’
261â†’    companion object {
262â†’        /**
263â†’         * Obtains the options for [AutomaticAnswer] in the deck config ("review" section)
264â†’         * @return null if the deck is dynamic (use global settings),
265â†’         * or if "useGeneralTimeoutSettings" is set
266â†’         */
267â†’        fun queryDeckSpecificOptions(
268â†’            action: AutomaticAnswerAction,
269â†’            col: Collection,
270â†’            selectedDid: DeckId
271â†’        ): AutomaticAnswerSettings? {
272â†’            // Dynamic don't have review options; attempt to get deck-specific auto-advance options
273â†’            // but be prepared to go with all default if it's a dynamic deck
274â†’            if (col.decks.isDyn(selectedDid)) {
275â†’                return null
276â†’            }
277â†’
278â†’            val revOptions = col.decks.confForDid(selectedDid).getJSONObject("rev")
279â†’
280â†’            if (revOptions.optBoolean("useGeneralTimeoutSettings", true)) {
281â†’                // we want to use the general settings, no need for per-deck settings
282â†’                return null
283â†’            }
284â†’
285â†’            val useTimer = revOptions.optBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
286â†’            val waitQuestionSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
287â†’            val waitAnswerSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
288â†’            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, useTimer, waitQuestionSecond, waitAnswerSecond)
289â†’        }
290â†’
291â†’        fun queryFromPreferences(preferences: SharedPreferences, action: AutomaticAnswerAction): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
292â†’            val prefUseTimer: Boolean = preferences.getBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
293â†’            val prefWaitQuestionSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
294â†’            val prefWaitAnswerSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
295â†’            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, prefUseTimer, prefWaitQuestionSecond, prefWaitAnswerSecond)
296â†’        }
297â†’
298â†’        fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
299â†’            // deck specific options take precedence over general (preference-based) options.
300â†’            // the action can only be set via preferences (but is stored in the collection).
301â†’            val action = getAction(col)
302â†’            return queryDeckSpecificOptions(action, col, col.decks.selected()) ?: queryFromPreferences(preferences, action)
303â†’        }
304â†’
305â†’        private fun getAction(col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerAction {
306â†’            return try {
307â†’                val value: Int = col.config.get(AutomaticAnswerAction.CONFIG_KEY) ?: return AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD
308â†’                AutomaticAnswerAction.fromPreferenceValue(value)
309â†’            } catch (e: Exception) {
310â†’                AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD
311â†’            }
312â†’        }
313â†’    }
314â†’}
315â†’
316â†’/**
317â†’ * Represents a value from [R.array.automatic_answer_values]/[R.array.automatic_answer_options]
318â†’ * Executed when answering a card (showing the question).
319â†’ */
320â†’enum class AutomaticAnswerAction(private val preferenceValue: Int) {
321â†’    /** Default: least invasive action */
322â†’    BURY_CARD(0),
323â†’    ANSWER_AGAIN(1),
324â†’    ANSWER_HARD(2),
325â†’    ANSWER_GOOD(3),
326â†’    ANSWER_EASY(4);
327â†’
328â†’    fun execute(reviewer: Reviewer) {
329â†’        val numberOfButtons = 4
330â†’        val actualAction = handleInvalidButtons(numberOfButtons)
331â†’        val action = actualAction.toCommand(numberOfButtons)
332â†’        Timber.i("Executing %s", action)
333â†’        reviewer.executeCommand(action)
334â†’    }
335â†’
336â†’    /** Handle **Hard/Easy** uf they don't appear */
337â†’    private fun handleInvalidButtons(numberOfButtons: Int): AutomaticAnswerAction {
338â†’        return when (this) {
339â†’            ANSWER_HARD -> if (AnswerButtons.canAnswerHard(numberOfButtons)) ANSWER_HARD else ANSWER_GOOD
340â†’            ANSWER_EASY -> if (AnswerButtons.canAnswerEasy(numberOfButtons)) ANSWER_EASY else ANSWER_GOOD
341â†’            // Again and Good always appear. So does Bury
342â†’            else -> this
343â†’        }
344â†’    }
345â†’
346â†’    /** Convert to a [ViewerCommand] */
347â†’    private fun toCommand(numberOfButtons: Int): ViewerCommand {
348â†’        return when (this) {
349â†’            BURY_CARD -> ViewerCommand.BURY_CARD
350â†’            ANSWER_AGAIN -> AGAIN.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
351â†’            ANSWER_HARD -> HARD.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
352â†’            ANSWER_GOOD -> GOOD.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
353â†’            ANSWER_EASY -> EASY.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
354â†’        }
355â†’    }
356â†’
357â†’    companion object {
358â†’        /**
359â†’         * An integer representing the action when Automatic Answer flips a card from answer to question
360â†’         *
361â†’         * 0 represents "bury", 1-4 represents the named buttons
362â†’         *
363â†’         * Although AnkiMobile and AnkiDroid have the feature, this config key is currently AnkiDroid only
364â†’         *
365â†’         * @see AutomaticAnswerAction
366â†’         */
367â†’        const val CONFIG_KEY = "automaticAnswerAction"
368â†’
369â†’        /** convert from [R.array.automatic_answer_values] ([R.array.automatic_answer_options]) to the enum */
370â†’        fun fromPreferenceValue(i: Int): AutomaticAnswerAction {
371â†’            return values().firstOrNull { it.preferenceValue == i } ?: BURY_CARD
372â†’        }
373â†’    }
374â†’}

 46â†’class Decks(private val col: Collection) {
 47â†’    /** "Add a deck with NAME. Reuse deck if already exists. Return id as int." */
 48â†’    fun id(name: String): DeckId {
 49â†’        val id = this.idForName(name)
 50â†’        if (id != null) {
 51â†’            return id
 52â†’        }
 53â†’        val deck = this.newDeckLegacy(false)
 54â†’        deck.name = name

 56â†’        return deck.id

27â†’class AnkiPackageImporterFragment : PageFragment() {
28â†’    override val title: String
29â†’        get() = resources.getString(R.string.menu_import)
30â†’    override val pageName: String
31â†’        get() = "import-page"
32â†’    override lateinit var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
33â†’    override var webChromeClient: PageChromeClient = PageChromeClient()
34â†’    private lateinit var backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
35â†’
36â†’    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
37â†’        // the back callback is only enabled when import is running and showing progress
38â†’        backCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
39â†’            override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
40â†’                CollectionManager.getBackend().setWantsAbort()
41â†’                // once triggered the callback is not needed as the import process can't be resumed
42â†’                remove()
43â†’            }
44â†’        }
45â†’        val path = arguments?.getString(ARG_FILE_PATH)
46â†’            ?: throw IllegalStateException("No path provided for apkg package to import")
47â†’        webViewClient = AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(path, backCallback)
48â†’        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
49â†’        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, backCallback)
50â†’    }
51â†’
52â†’    class AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(
53â†’        private val path: String,
54â†’        private val backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
55â†’    ) : PageWebViewClient() {
56â†’        /**
57â†’         * Ideally, to handle the state of the back callback, we would just need to check for
58â†’         * `/latestProgress` calls followed by one `/importDone` call. However there are some extra
59â†’         * calls to `/latestProgress` AFTER `/importDone` and this property keeps track of this.
60â†’         */
61â†’        private var isDone = false
62â†’
63â†’        override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
64â†’            val params = """{ type: "json_file", path: "$path"}"""
65â†’            // https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/main/ts/import-page/index.ts
66â†’            view!!.evaluateJavascript("anki.setupImportPage($params);$hideShowButtonCss;") {
67â†’                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
68â†’            }
69â†’        }
70â†’
71â†’        override fun onLoadResource(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
72â†’            super.onLoadResource(view, url)
73â†’            backCallback.isEnabled = when {
74â†’                url == null -> false
75â†’                url.endsWith("latestProgress") && !isDone -> true
76â†’                url.endsWith("importDone") -> {
77â†’                    isDone = true // import was done so disable any back callback changes after this call
78â†’                    false
79â†’                }
80â†’                else -> false
81â†’            }
82â†’        }
83â†’    }
84â†’
85â†’    companion object {
86â†’        private const val ARG_FILE_PATH = "arg_file_path"
87â†’
88â†’        fun getIntent(context: Context, filePath: String): Intent {
89â†’            val args = bundleOf(ARG_FILE_PATH to filePath)
90â†’            return SingleFragmentActivity.getIntent(context, AnkiPackageImporterFragment::class, args)
91â†’        }
92â†’    }
93â†’}

29â†’/**
30â†’ * Base class for displaying Anki HTML pages
31â†’ */
32â†’@Suppress("LeakingThis")
33â†’abstract class PageFragment : Fragment(), PostRequestHandler {
34â†’    abstract val title: String
35â†’    abstract val pageName: String
36â†’    abstract var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
37â†’    abstract var webChromeClient: PageChromeClient
38â†’
39â†’    lateinit var webView: WebView
40â†’    private val server = AnkiServer(this).also { it.start() }
41â†’
42â†’    override fun onCreateView(
43â†’        inflater: LayoutInflater,
44â†’        container: ViewGroup?,
45â†’        savedInstanceState: Bundle?
46â†’    ): View? {
47â†’        val view = inflater.inflate(R.layout.page_fragment, container, false)
48â†’
49â†’        webView = view.findViewById<WebView>(R.id.webview).apply {
50â†’            with(settings) {
51â†’                javaScriptEnabled = true
52â†’                displayZoomControls = false
53â†’                builtInZoomControls = true
54â†’                setSupportZoom(true)
55â†’            }
56â†’            webViewClient = this@PageFragment.webViewClient
57â†’            webChromeClient = this@PageFragment.webChromeClient
58â†’        }
59â†’        val nightMode = if (Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode) "#night" else ""
60â†’        val url = server.baseUrl() + "$pageName.html$nightMode"
61â†’
62â†’        Timber.i("Loading $url")
63â†’        webView.loadUrl(url)
64â†’
65â†’        view.findViewById<MaterialToolbar>(R.id.toolbar).apply {
66â†’            title = this@PageFragment.title
67â†’            setNavigationOnClickListener {
68â†’                requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
69â†’            }
70â†’        }
71â†’        return view
72â†’    }
73â†’
74â†’    override suspend fun handlePostRequest(uri: String, bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
75â†’        val methodName = if (uri.startsWith(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX)) {
76â†’            uri.substring(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX.length)
77â†’        } else {
78â†’            throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $uri")
79â†’        }
80â†’        return requireActivity().handleUiPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
81â†’            ?: handleCollectionPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
82â†’            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled method: $methodName")
83â†’    }
84â†’}

26â†’fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
27â†’    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
28â†’    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
29â†’        .setHttpAllowed(true)
30â†’        .setDomain(domain)
31â†’        .addPathHandler("/web/") { path: String ->
32â†’            val inputStream = this.javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream("web/$path")
33â†’            WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
34â†’        }
35â†’        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
36â†’            try {
37â†’                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
38â†’                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
39â†’                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
40â†’            } catch (e: Exception) {
41â†’                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
42â†’                null
43â†’            }
44â†’        }
45â†’        .build()
