30â†’suspend fun importJsonFileRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
31â†’    return withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
32â†’        val output = withCol { this.importAnkiPackageRaw(input) }
33â†’        val changes = OpChangesOnly.parseFrom(output)
34â†’        undoableOp { changes }
35â†’        output
36â†’    }
37â†’}
38â†’
39â†’suspend fun importCsvRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
40â†’    return withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
41â†’        val output = withCol { importCsvRaw(input) }
42â†’        val changes = OpChangesOnly.parseFrom(output)
43â†’        undoableOp { changes }
44â†’        output
45â†’    }
46â†’}
47â†’
48â†’/**
49â†’ * Css to hide the "Show" button from the final backend import page. As the user could import a lot
50â†’ * of notes, on pressing "Show" the native CardBrowser would be called with a search query
51â†’ * comprising of all the notes ids. This would result in a crash or very slow behavior in the
52â†’ * CardBrowser.
53â†’ *
54â†’ * NOTE: this should be used only with [android.webkit.WebView.evaluateJavascript].
55â†’ */
56â†’val hideShowButtonCss = """
57â†’    javascript:(
58â†’        function() {
59â†’            var hideShowButtonStyle = '.desktop-only { display: none !important; }';
60â†’            var newStyle = document.createElement('style');                    
61â†’            newStyle.appendChild(document.createTextNode(hideShowButtonStyle));
62â†’            document.head.appendChild(newStyle);       
63â†’        }
64â†’    )()
65â†’""".trimIndent()
66â†’
67â†’/**
68â†’ * Calls the native [CardBrowser] to display the results of the search query constructed from the
69â†’ * input. This method will always return the received input.
70â†’ */
71â†’suspend fun FragmentActivity.searchInBrowser(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
72â†’    val searchString = withCol { buildSearchString(input) }
73â†’    val starterIntent = Intent(this, CardBrowser::class.java).apply {
74â†’        putExtra("search_query", searchString)
75â†’        putExtra("all_decks", true)
76â†’    }
77â†’    startActivity(starterIntent)
78â†’    return input
79â†’}

 294â†’    private fun selectDeckFromExtra() {
 295â†’        val extras = intent.extras
 296â†’        if (extras == null || !extras.containsKey("deckId")) {
 297â†’            // deckId is not set, load default
 298â†’            return
 299â†’        }
 300â†’        val did = extras.getLong("deckId", Long.MIN_VALUE)
 301â†’        Timber.d("selectDeckFromExtra() with deckId = %d", did)
 302â†’
 303â†’        // deckId does not exist, load default
 304â†’        if (getColUnsafe.decks.get(did) == null) {
 305â†’            Timber.w("selectDeckFromExtra() deckId '%d' doesn't exist", did)
 306â†’            return
 307â†’        }
 308â†’        // Select the deck
 309â†’        getColUnsafe.decks.select(did)
 310â†’    }

1382â†’    override suspend fun handlePostRequest(uri: String, bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
1383â†’        return if (uri.startsWith(ANKI_PREFIX)) {
1384â†’            when (val methodName = uri.substring(ANKI_PREFIX.length)) {
1385â†’                "getSchedulingStatesWithContext" -> getSchedulingStatesWithContext()
1386â†’                "setSchedulingStates" -> setSchedulingStates(bytes)
1387â†’                else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $methodName")
1388â†’            }
1389â†’        } else if (uri.startsWith(ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX)) {
1390â†’            jsApi.handleJsApiRequest(uri.substring(ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX.length), bytes, true)
1391â†’        } else {
1392â†’            throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $uri")
1393â†’        }
1394â†’    }

1396â†’    private fun getSchedulingStatesWithContext(): ByteArray {
1397â†’        val state = queueState ?: return ByteArray(0)
1398â†’        return state.schedulingStatesWithContext().toBuilder()
1399â†’            .mergeStates(
1400â†’                state.states.toBuilder().mergeCurrent(
1401â†’                    state.states.current.toBuilder()
1402â†’                        .setCustomData(state.topCard.toBackendCard().customData).build()
1403â†’                ).build()
1404â†’            )
1405â†’            .build()
1406â†’            .toByteArray()
1407â†’    }

1409â†’    private fun setSchedulingStates(bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
1410â†’        val state = queueState
1411â†’        if (state == null) {
1412â†’            statesMutated = true
1413â†’            return ByteArray(0)
1414â†’        }
1415â†’        val req = SetSchedulingStatesRequest.parseFrom(bytes)
1416â†’        if (req.key == customSchedulingKey) {
1417â†’            state.states = req.states
1418â†’        }
1419â†’        statesMutated = true
1420â†’        return ByteArray(0)
1421â†’    }

1423â†’    private fun createWhiteboard() {

1425â†’

 55â†’        // The version number is of the form:
 56â†’        // <major>.<minor>.<maintenance>[dev|alpha<build>|beta<build>|]
 57â†’        // The <build> is only present for alpha and beta releases (e.g., 2.0.4alpha2 or 2.0.4beta4), developer builds do
 58â†’        // not have a build number (e.g., 2.0.4dev) and official releases only have three components (e.g., 2.0.4).
 59â†’        //
 60â†’        // The version code is derived from the version name as follows:
 61â†’        // AbbCCtDD
 62â†’        // A: 1-digit decimal number representing the major version
 63â†’        // bb: 2-digit decimal number representing the minor version
 64â†’        // CC: 2-digit decimal number representing the maintenance version
 65â†’        // t: 1-digit decimal number representing the type of the build
 66â†’        // 0: developer build
 67â†’        // 1: alpha release
 68â†’        // 2: beta release
 69â†’        // 3: public release
 70â†’        // DD: 2-digit decimal number representing the build
 71â†’        // 00 for internal builds and public releases
 72â†’        // alpha/beta build number for alpha/beta releases
 73â†’        //
 74â†’        // This ensures the correct ordering between the various types of releases (dev < alpha < beta < release) which is
 75â†’        // needed for upgrades to be offered correctly.
 76â†’        versionCode=21800105

174â†’    /**
175â†’     * Set this to true to create five separate APKs instead of one:
176â†’     *   - 2 APKs that only work on ARM/ARM64 devices
177â†’     *   - 2 APKs that only works on x86/x86_64 devices
178â†’     *   - a universal APK that works on all devices
179â†’     * The advantage is the size of most APKs is reduced by about 2.5MB.
180â†’     * Upload all the APKs to the Play Store and people will download
181â†’     * the correct one based on the CPU architecture of their device.
182â†’     */
183â†’    def enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture = true
184â†’
185â†’    splits {
186â†’        abi {
187â†’            reset()
188â†’            enable enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture
189â†’            //universalApk enableUniversalApk  // set in debug + release config blocks above
190â†’            include "armeabi-v7a", "x86", "arm64-v8a", "x86_64"
191â†’        }
192â†’    }
193â†’    // applicationVariants are e.g. debug, release
194â†’    applicationVariants.configureEach { variant ->
195â†’        // We want the same version stream for all ABIs in debug but for release we can split them
196â†’        if (variant.buildType.name == 'release') {
197â†’            variant.outputs.configureEach { output ->
198â†’
199â†’                // For each separate APK per architecture, set a unique version code as described here:
200â†’                // https://developer.android.com/studio/build/configure-apk-splits.html
201â†’                def versionCodes = ["armeabi-v7a": 1, "x86": 2, "arm64-v8a": 3, "x86_64": 4]
202â†’                def outputFile = output.outputFile
203â†’                if (outputFile != null && outputFile.name.endsWith('.apk')) {
204â†’                    def abi = output.getFilter("ABI")
205â†’                    if (abi != null) {  // null for the universal-debug, universal-release variants
206â†’                        //  From: https://developer.android.com/studio/publish/versioning#appversioning
207â†’                        //  "Warning: The greatest value Google Play allows for versionCode is 2100000000"
208â†’                        //  AnkiDroid versionCodes have a budget 8 digits (through AnkiDroid 9)
209â†’                        //  This style does ABI version code ranges with the 9th digit as 0-4.
210â†’                        //  This consumes ~20% of the version range space, w/50 years of versioning at our major-version pace
211â†’                        output.versionCodeOverride =
212â†’                                // ex:  321200106 = 3 * 100000000 + 21200106
213â†’                                versionCodes.get(abi) * 100000000 + defaultConfig.versionCode
214â†’                    }
215â†’                }
216â†’            }
217â†’        }
218â†’    }
219â†’

225â†’            ]
226â†’        }
227â†’    }

260â†’// Issue 11078 - some emulators run, but run zero tests, and still report success
261â†’tasks.register('assertNonzeroAndroidTests') {
262â†’    doLast {
263â†’        // androidTest currently creates one .xml file per emulator with aggregate results in this dir
264â†’        File folder = file("./build/outputs/androidTest-results/connected/flavors/play")
265â†’        File[] listOfFiles = folder.listFiles({ d, f -> f ==~ /.*.xml/ } as FilenameFilter)
266â†’        for (File file : listOfFiles) {
267â†’            // The aggregate results file currently contains a line with this pattern holding test count
268â†’            String[] matches = file.readLines().findAll { it.contains('<testsuite') }
269â†’            if (matches.length != 1) {
270â†’                throw new GradleScriptException("Unable to determine count of tests executed for " + file.name + ". Regex pattern out of date?", null)
271â†’            }
272â†’            if (!(matches[0] ==~ /.* tests="\d+" .*/) || matches[0].contains('tests="0"')) {
273â†’                throw new GradleScriptException("androidTest executed 0 tests for " + file.name + " - Probably a bug with the emulator. Try another image.", null)
274â†’            }
275â†’        }
276â†’    }
277â†’}
278â†’afterEvaluate {
279â†’    tasks.named('connectedPlayDebugAndroidTest').configure { finalizedBy('assertNonzeroAndroidTests') }
280â†’}
